title: CommandEvent Object
description: An event endpoint that supports the connection to client implemented CommandEventHandlers.

methods.name: add
methods.description: Adds an event handler object to this event endpoint.

methods.name: classType
methods.description: Static function that all classes support that returns the type of the class as a string. The returned string matches the string returned by the objectType property. For example if you have a reference to an object and you want to check if it's a SketchLine you can use myObject.objectType == fusion.SketchLine.classType().

methods.name: remove
methods.description: Removes a handler from this event endpoint.

properties.name: isValid
properties.description: Indicates if this object is still valid, i.e. hasn't been deleted or some other action done to invalidate the reference.

properties.name: name
properties.description: The name of the event - e.g. "DocumentOpening"

properties.name: objectType
properties.description: This property is supported by all objects in the API and returns a string that contains the full name (namespace::objecttype) describing the type of the object.   It's often useful to use this in combination with the classType method to see if an object is a certain type. For example: if obj.objectType == adsk.core.Point3D.classType():

properties.name: sender
properties.description: The object that is firing the event. For example, in the case of a command input event this will return the command.

related_samples.name: Command Inputs API Sample
related_samples.description:
Creates a command dialog that demonstrates all of the available command inputs.

To use the sample, create a new Python or C++ script and copy and paste this code, replacing the default code.  You also need to unpack this zip file which contains a resource folder into the same folder where the source code file (.py or .cpp) is.

add.title: CommandEvent.add Method
add.description: Adds an event handler object to this event endpoint.
add.return_description: Returns true if the handler was successfully added to the set of event handlers.

parameters.name: handler
parameters.description: The client implemented CommandEventHandler to be called when this event is triggered.

related_samples.name: Command Inputs API Sample
related_samples.description:
Creates a command dialog that demonstrates all of the available command inputs.

To use the sample, create a new Python or C++ script and copy and paste this code, replacing the default code. You also need to unpack this zip file which contains a resource folder into the same folder where the source code file (.py or .cpp) is.

classType.title: CommandEvent.classType Method
classType.description: Static function that all classes support that returns the type of the class as a string. The returned string matches the string returned by the objectType property. For example if you have a reference to an object and you want to check if it's a SketchLine you can use myObject.objectType == fusion.SketchLine.classType().
classType.return_description: Returns a string indicating the type of the object.

remove.title: CommandEvent.remove Method
remove.description: Removes a handler from this event endpoint.
remove.return_description: Returns true if the handler was found and removed from the set of event handlers.

parameters.name: handler
parameters.description: A CommandEventHandler that was previously added to this event with the add method.

isValid.title: CommandEvent.isValid Property
isValid.description: Indicates if this object is still valid, i.e. hasn't been deleted or some other action done to invalidate the reference.

syntax.python:
# Get the value of the property.
propertyValue = commandEvent_var.isValid

name.title: CommandEvent.name Property
name.description: The name of the event - e.g. "DocumentOpening"

syntax.python:
# Get the value of the property.
propertyValue = commandEvent_var.name

objectType.title: CommandEvent.objectType Property
objectType.description: This property is supported by all objects in the API and returns a string that contains the full name (namespace::objecttype) describing the type of the object. It's often useful to use this in combination with the classType method to see if an object is a certain type. For example: if obj.objectType == adsk.core.Point3D.classType():

syntax.python:
# Get the value of the property.
propertyValue = commandEvent_var.objectType

sender.title: CommandEvent.sender Property
sender.description: The object that is firing the event. For example, in the case of a command input event this will return the command.

syntax.python:
# Get the value of the property.
propertyValue = commandEvent_var.sender
